pred_reason: The SWC-101 label is reasonable because multiple arithmetic operations lack overflow/underflow protection, including critical underflows in unregisterToken and general arithmetic vulnerabilities across functions. These issues can lead to contract malfunction and potential storage corruption, directly aligning with SWC-101's scope of integer overflow and underflow risks. Evidence: unregisterToken uses addresses[pos-1] without bounds check, risking underflow if pos=0; getTokens performs start+count and i-start operations without overflow safeguards; addresses.length-- in unregisterToken lacks underflow protection for the array length

findings.Description: The registerMintedToken function lacks the onlyOwner modifier, allowing any external address to register tokens. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Unauthorized token registration, potential system pollution, phishing attacks, and loss of registry integrity

findings.Description: Multiple arithmetic operations lack overflow/underflow protection in Solidity 0.4.21. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Contract malfunction, incorrect state management, potential denial of service, and storage corruption

findings.Description: addresses[pos-1] can underflow if pos=0, leading to arbitrary storage access. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Arbitrary storage write, potential contract takeover, and complete system compromise

findings.Description: areAllTokensRegistered and getTokens functions contain loops that can consume excessive gas as arrays grow. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Denial-of-service through out-of-gas errors, unreliable external integrations

findings.Description: The Claimable ownership transfer process is vulnerable to front-running attacks. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Malicious actors can hijack ownership transfers and gain contract control
